title: arum orientale subsp. incomptum
intro: Arum orientale subsp. incomptum, also known as the wild arum or dragon arum, is a plant species in the Arum genus, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of calcium oxalate crystals and other compounds, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Arum orientale subsp. incomptum include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in traditional medicine to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
